           stateListener.onStateChange(oldState, newState, startTime());  // Callback within lock!\n        }\n    }\n}\n\npublic boolean stillRunning() {\n    synchronized (stateLock) {  // Line 188-190\n        return state.isRunning();\n    }\n}\n\npublic boolean inErrorState() {\n    synchronized (stateLock) {  // Line 194-196\n        return state.inErrorState();\n    }\n}\n\n@Override\npublic synchronized void start() {  // Line 454\n    super.start();\n    try {\n        initializationLatch.await();  // POTENTIAL DEADLOCK: await() while holding method's intrinsic lock\n    } catch (final InterruptedException e) {\n        currentThread().interrupt();\n        throw new IllegalStateException(\"GlobalStreamThread was interrupted during initialization\", e);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**CRITICAL ISSUE at Line 454-461:** The `start()` method is synchronized but calls `initializationLatch.await()`. While `CountDownLatch.await()` doesn't use a lock, holding the intrinsic lock on `this` while blocking could prevent other threads from calling synchronized methods on this object.\n\n---\n\n## **3. SUMMARY TABLE: Potential Deadlock/Missed Signal Scenarios**\n\n| File | Issue Type | Lock/Condition | Line(s) | Risk Level |\n|------|-----------|---------------|---------|-----------|\n| DefaultStateUpdater.java | Multiple lock acquisition | 3 ReentrantLocks | 1088-1099 | HIGH |\n| DefaultStateUpdater.java | Missed signal | tasksAndActionsCondition | 446-459 | MEDIUM |\n| DefaultStateUpdater.java | Nested lock without ordering | restoredActiveTasksLock, updatingTasks | 710-719 | MEDIUM |\n| DefaultTaskManager.java | Missed signal on shutdown | tasksCondition | 154-188 | MEDIUM |\n| TopologyMetadata.java | Missed signal (race) | topologyCV | 264-278 | MEDIUM |\n| SynchronizedPartitionGroup.java | Coarse-grained synchronization | implicit lock | 35-102 | LOW |\n| ThreadCache.java | Nested synchronized blocks | synchronized(cache) within synchronized method | 80-100, 176-181 | HIGH |\n| GlobalStreamThread.java | Blocking await under lock | intrinsic lock + CountDownLatch | 454-461 | MEDIUM |\n\n---\n\n## **4. 45→import java.util.stream.Collectors;\n    46→\n    47→/**\n    48→ * An active task could only be in one of the following status:\n    49→ *\n    50→ * 1. It's assigned to one of the executors for processing.\n    51→ * 2. It's locked for committing, removal, other manipulations etc.\n    52→ * 3. Neither 1 or 2, i.e. it stays idle. This is possible if we do not have enough executors or because those tasks\n    53→ *    are not processable (e.g. because no records fetched) yet.\n    54→ */\n    55→public final class DefaultTaskManager implements TaskManager {\n    56→\n    57→    private final Time time;\n    58→    private final Logger log;\n    59→    private final TasksRegistry tasks;\n    60→    private final TopologyMetadata topologyMetadata;\n    61→\n    62→    private final Lock tasksLock = new ReentrantLock();\n    63→    private final Condition tasksCondition = tasksLock.newCondition();\n    64→    private final List<TaskId> lockedTasks = new ArrayList<>();\n    65→    private final Map<TaskId, StreamsException> uncaughtExceptions = new HashMap<>();\n    66→    private final Map<TaskId, TaskExecutor> assignedTasks = new HashMap<>();\n    67→    private final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata;\n    68→\n    69→    private final List<TaskExecutor> taskExecutors;\n    70→\n    71→    public static class DefaultTaskExecutorCreator implements TaskExecutorCreator {\n    72→        @Override\n    73→        public TaskExecutor create(final TaskManager taskManager, final String name, final Time time, final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata) {\n    74→            return new DefaultTaskExecutor(taskManager, name, time, taskExecutionMetadata);\n    75→        }\n      final int numExecutors,\n    84→                              final TopologyMetadata topologyMetadata\n    85→                              ) {\n    86→        final String logPrefix =       }\n   100→    }\n   101→\n   102→    private class TaskTopologyObserver implements TopologyMetadata.TopologyLifecycleObserver {\n   103→        @Override\n   104→        public void private void validateTopologyConsistency(final Set<String> activeTopologies) {\n   110→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   111→            for (final StreamTask task : !activeTopologies.contains(topologyName)) {\n   114→                    log.warn(\"Task {} belongs to topology {} which is no longer active\", task.id(), topologyName);\n   115→                }\n   116→            }\n   117→        });\n   118→    }\n   119→\n   120→    @Override\n   121→    public StreamTask assignNextTask(final TaskExecutor executor) {\n   122→        return assign is unrecognized\");\n   125→            }\n   126→\n   127→            // the most naive scheduling algorithm for now: give the next unlocked, unassigned, and  processable task\n   128→            for (final StreamTask task : tasks.activeInitializedTasks()) {\n   129→                if (topologyMetadata.isTaskTopologyStale(task.id())) {\n   130→                    continue;\n   131→                }\n   132→                if (!assignedTasks.containsKey(task.id()) &&\n   133→                    !lockedTasks.contains(task.id()) &&\n   134→                    canProgress(task, }\n   145→\n   146→            log.debug(\"Found no assignable task for executor {}\", executor.name());\n   147→\n   148→            return null;\n   149→        });\n   150→    }\n   151→\n   152→    @Override\n   153→    public void awaitProcessableTasks(final Supplier<Boolean> isShuttingDown) throws InterruptedException {\n   154→        final boolean interrupted = returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   155→            for (final StreamTask task : tasks.activeInitializedTasks()) {\n   156→                if (!assignedTasks.containsKey(task.id()) &&\n   157→                    !lockedTasks.contains(task.id()) &&\n   158→                    canProgress(task, time.milliseconds()) &&\n   159→                    !hasUncaughtException(task.id())\n   160→                ) {\n   161→                    log.debug(\"Await unblocked: returning early from await since a processable task {} was found\", task.id());\n   162→                    return false;\n   163→                }\n   164→            }\n   165→            try {\n   166→                // We re-check the shutdownRequest atomic boolean to avoid a race condition. If this thread was\n   167→                // previously interrupted while awaiting tasksCondition, it is possible to miss the signalAll that\n   168→                // is called during shutdown. log.debug(\"Await unblocked: Interrupted while waiting for processable tasks\");\n   179→                return true;\n   180→            }\n   181→            log.debug(\"Await unblocked: Woken up to }\n   188→    }\n   189→\n   190→    public void signalTaskExecutors() {\n   191→        log.debug(\"Waking up task executors\");\n   192→        executeWithTasksLocked(tasksCondition::signalAll);\n   193→    }\n   194→\n   195→    @Override\n   196→    public void unassignTask(final StreamTask task, final TaskExecutor executor) {\n   197→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   198→ public KafkaFuture<Void> lockTasks(final Set<TaskId> taskIds) {\n   216→        final KafkaFutureImpl<Void> result = new KafkaFutureImpl<>();\n   217→\n   218→        if (taskIds.isEmpty()) {\n   219→            result.complete(null);\n   220→            return result;\n   221→        }\n   222→\n   223→        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   224→            lockedTasks.addAll(taskIds);\n 253→                                result.complete(null);\n   254→                            }\n   255→                        }\n   256→                    });\n   257→                } else {\n   258→                    remainingTaskIds.remove(taskId);\n   259→                    if (remainingTaskIds.isEmpty()) {\n   260→                        result.complete(null);\n   261→ {\n   363→            log.debug(\"Drained {} uncaught exceptions\", returnValue.size());\n   364→        }\n   365→\n   366→        return returnValue;\n   367→    }\n   368→\n   369→    public boolean hasUncaughtException(final TaskId taskId) {\n   370→        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> uncaughtExceptions.containsKey(taskId));\n   371→    }\n   372→\n   373→    private void executeWithTasksLocked(final Runnable action) {\n   374→        tasksLock.lock();\n   375→        try {\n   376→            action.run();\n   377→        } finally {\n   378→            tasksLock.unlock();\n   379→        }\n   380→    }\n   381→\n   382→    private <T> T returnWithTasksLocked(final Supplier<T> action) {\n   383→        tasksLock.lock();\n   384→        try {\n   385→            return action.get();\n   386→        } finally {\n   387→            tasksLock.unlock();\n   388→        }\n   389→    }\n   390→\n   391→    private boolean canProgress(final StreamTask task, final long nowMs) {\n   392→        return\n   393→            taskExecutionMetadata.canProcessTask(task, nowMs) && task.isProcessable(nowMs) ||\n   394→                taskExecutionMetadata.canPunctuateTask(task) && (task.canPunctuateStreamTime() || task.canPunctuateSystemTime());\n   395→    }\n   396→\n   397→    public void startTaskExecutors() {\n   398→        for (final TaskExecutor t: taskExecutors) {\n   399→            t.start();\n   400→        }\n   401→    }\n   402→\n   403→    public void shutdown(final Duration duration) {\n   404→        for (final TaskExecutor t: taskExecutors) {\n   405→            t.requestShutdown();\n   406→        }\n   407→        signalTaskExecutors();\n   408→        for (final TaskExecutor t: taskExecutors) {\n   409→            t.awaitShutdown(duration);\n   410→        }\n   411→    }\n   412→}\n   413→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a146cae9-d397-4d03-b566-1cf292ffa421","uuid":"ab4e2794-11c1-43a3-89d6-35f67f99464b","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/tasks/DefaultTaskManager.java","content":"/*\n * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n *\n *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n *\n * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\npackage org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.tasks;\n\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaFuture;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.internals.KafkaFutureImpl;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.LogContext;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Time;\nimport org.apache.kafka.streams.errors.StreamsException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.TaskId;\nimport org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.ReadOnlyTask;\nimport org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.StreamTask;\nimport org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.Task;\nimport org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.TaskExecutionMetadata;\nimport org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.TasksRegistry;\nimport org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.TopologyMetadata;\n\nimport org.slf4j.Logger;\n\nimport java.time.Duration;\nimport java.util.ArrayList;\nimport java.util.HashMap;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.Map;\nimport java.util.Set;\nimport java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentSkipListSet;\nimport java.util.concurrent.locks.Condition;\nimport java.util.concurrent.locks.Lock;\nimport java.util.concurrent.locks.ReentrantLock;\nimport java.util.function.Supplier;\nimport java.util.stream.Collectors;\n\n/**\n * An active task could only be in one of the following status:\n *\n * 1. It's assigned to one of the executors for processing.\n * 2. It's locked for committing, removal, other manipulations etc.\n * 3. Neither 1 or 2, i.e. it stays idle. This is possible if we do not have enough executors or because those tasks\n *    are not processable (e.g. because no records fetched) yet.\n */\npublic final class DefaultTaskManager implements TaskManager {\n\n    private final Time time;\n    private final Logger log;\n    private final TasksRegistry tasks;\n    private final TopologyMetadata topologyMetadata;\n\n    private final Lock tasksLock = new ReentrantLock();\n    private final Condition tasksCondition = tasksLock.newCondition();\n    private final List<TaskId> lockedTasks = new ArrayList<>();\n    private final Map<TaskId, StreamsException> uncaughtExceptions = new HashMap<>();\n    private final Map<TaskId, TaskExecutor> assignedTasks = new HashMap<>();\n    private final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata;\n\n    private final List<TaskExecutor> taskExecutors;\n\n    public static class DefaultTaskExecutorCreator implements TaskExecutorCreator {\n        @Override\n        public TaskExecutor create(final TaskManager taskManager, final String name, final Time time, final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata) {\n            return new DefaultTaskExecutor(taskManager, name, time, taskExecutionMetadata);\n        }\n    }\n\n    public DefaultTaskManager(final Time time,\n                              final String clientId,\n                              final TasksRegistry tasks,\n                              final TaskExecutorCreator executorCreator,\n                              final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata,\n                              final int numExecutors,\n                              final TopologyMetadata topologyMetadata\n                              ) {\n        final String logPrefix = String.format(\"%s \", clientId);\n        final LogContext logContext = new LogContext(logPrefix);\n        this.log = logContext.logger(DefaultTaskManager.class);\n        this.time = time;\n        this.tasks = tasks;\n        this.taskExecutionMetadata = taskExecutionMetadata;\n        this.topologyMetadata = topologyMetadata;\n        topologyMetadata.registerLifecycleObserver(new TaskTopologyObserver());\n\n        this.taskExecutors = new ArrayList<>(numExecutors);\n        for (int i = 1; i <= numExecutors; i++) {\n            final String name = clientId + \"-TaskExecutor-\" + i;\n            this.taskExecutors.add(executorCreator.create(this, name, time, taskExecutionMetadata));\n        }\n    }\n\n    private class TaskTopologyObserver implements TopologyMetadata.TopologyLifecycleObserver {\n        @Override\n        public void onTopologyChange(final long newVersion, final Set<String> activeTopologies) {\n            validateTopologyConsistency(activeTopologies);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void validateTopologyConsistency(final Set<String> activeTopologies) {\n        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n            for (final StreamTask task : tasks.activeInitializedTasks()) {\n                final String topologyName = task.id().topologyName();\n                if (topologyName != null && !activeTopologies.contains(topologyName)) {\n                    log.warn(\"Task {} belongs to topology {} which is no longer active\", task.id(), topologyName);\n                }\n            }\n        });\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public StreamTask assignNextTask(final TaskExecutor executor) {\n        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n            if (!taskExecutors.contains(executor)) {\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The requested executor for getting next task to assign is unrecognized\");\n            }\n\n            // the most naive scheduling algorithm for now: give the next unlocked, unassigned, and  processable task\n            for (final StreamTask task : tasks.activeInitializedTasks()) {\n                if (topologyMetadata.isTaskTopologyStale(task.id())) {\n                    continue;\n                }\n                if (!assignedTasks.containsKey(task.id()) &&\n                    !lockedTasks.contains(task.id()) &&\n                    canProgress(task, time.milliseconds()) &&\n                    !hasUncaughtException(task.id())\n                ) {\n\n                    assignedTasks.put(task.id(), executor);\n\n                    log.debug(\"Assigned task {} to executor {}\", task.id(), executor.name());\n\n                    return task;\n                }\n            }\n\n            log.debug(\"Found no assignable task for executor {}\", executor.name());\n\n            return null;\n        });\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void awaitProcessableTasks(final Supplier<Boolean> isShuttingDown) throws InterruptedException {\n        final boolean interrupted = returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n            for (final StreamTask task : tasks.activeInitializedTasks()) {\n                if (!assignedTasks.containsKey(task.id()) &&\n                    !lockedTasks.contains(task.id()) &&\n                    canProgress(task, time.milliseconds()) &&\n                    !hasUncaughtException(task.id())\n                ) {\n                    log.debug(\"Await unblocked: returning early from await since a processable task {} was found\", task.id());\n                    return false;\n                }\n            }\n            try {\n                // We re-check the shutdownRequest atomic boolean to avoid a race condition. If this thread was\n                // previously interrupted while awaiting tasksCondition, it is possible to miss the signalAll that\n                // is called during shutdown. If this happens, we end up blocking in the await forever.\n                if (!isShuttingDown.get()) {\n                    log.debug(\"Await blocking\");\n                    tasksCondition.await();\n                } else {\n                    log.debug(\"Not awaiting since shutdown was requested\");\n                }\n            } catch (final InterruptedException ignored) {\n                // we interrupt the thread for shut down and pause.\n                // we can ignore this exception.\n                log.debug(\"Await unblocked: Interrupted while waiting for processable tasks\");\n                return true;\n            }\n            log.debug(\"Await unblocked: Woken up to check for processable tasks\");\n            return false;\n        });\n\n        if (interrupted) {\n            throw new InterruptedException();\n        }\n    }\n\n    public void signalTaskExecutors() {\n        log.debug(\"Waking up task executors\");\n        executeWithTasksLocked(tasksCondition::signalAll);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void unassignTask(final StreamTask task, final TaskExecutor executor) {\n        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n            if (!taskExecutors.contains(executor)) {\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The requested executor for unassign task is unrecognized\");\n            }\n\n            final TaskExecutor lockedExecutor = assignedTasks.get(task.id());\n            if (lockedExecutor == null || lockedExecutor != executor) {\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Task \" + task.id() + \" is not locked by the executor\");\n            }\n\n            assignedTasks.remove(task.id());\n\n            log.debug(\"Unassigned {} from executor {}\", task.id(), executor.name());\n            tasksCondition.signalAll();\n        });\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public KafkaFuture<Void> lockTasks(final Set<TaskId> taskIds) {\n        final KafkaFutureImpl<Void> result = new KafkaFutureImpl<>();\n\n        if (taskIds.isEmpty()) {\n            result.complete(null);\n            return result;\n        }\n\n        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n            lockedTasks.addAll(taskIds);\n\n            final Set<TaskId> remainingTaskIds = new ConcurrentSkipListSet<>(taskIds);\n\n            for (final TaskId taskId : taskIds) {\n                final Task task = tasks.initializedTask(taskId);\n\n                if (task == null) {\n                    throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Trying to lock task \" + taskId + \" but it's not owned\");\n                }\n\n                if (!task.isActive()) {\n                    throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The locking task \" + taskId + \" is not an active task\");\n                }\n\n                if (assignedTasks.containsKey(taskId)) {\n                    final TaskExecutor executor = assignedTasks.get(taskId);\n                    log.debug(\"Requesting release of task {} from {}\", taskId, executor.name());\n                    final KafkaFuture<StreamTask> future = executor.unassign();\n                    future.whenComplete((streamTask, throwable) -> {\n                        if (throwable != null) {\n                            result.completeExceptionally(throwable);\n                        } else {\n                            assert !assignedTasks.containsKey(taskId);\n                            // It can happen that the executor handed back the task before we asked it to\n                            // in which case `streamTask` will be null here.\n                            assert streamTask == null || streamTask.id() == taskId;\n                            remainingTaskIds.remove(taskId);\n                            if (remainingTaskIds.isEmpty()) {\n                                result.complete(null);\n                            }\n                        }\n                    });\n                } else {\n                    remainingTaskIds.remove(taskId);\n                    if (remainingTaskIds.isEmpty()) {\n                        result.complete(null);\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            return result;\n        });\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public KafkaFuture<Void> lockAllTasks() {\n        return returnWithTasksLocked(() ->\n            lockTasks(tasks.activeInitializedTasks().stream().map(Task::id).collect(Collectors.toSet()))\n        );\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void unlockTasks(final Set<TaskId> taskIds) {\n\n        if (taskIds.isEmpty()) {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n            lockedTasks.removeAll(taskIds);\n            log.debug(\"Waking up task executors\");\n            tasksCondition.signalAll();\n        });\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void unlockAllTasks() {\n        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> unlockTasks(tasks.activeInitializedTasks().stream().map(Task::id).collect(Collectors.toSet())));\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void add(final Set<StreamTask> tasksToAdd) {\n        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n            for (final StreamTask task : tasksToAdd) {\n                tasks.addActiveTask(task);\n            }\n            log.debug(\"Waking up task executors\");\n            tasksCondition.signalAll();\n        });\n\n        log.info(\"Added tasks {} to the task manager to process\", tasksToAdd);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void remove(final TaskId taskId) {\n        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n            if (assignedTasks.containsKey(taskId)) {\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The task to remove is still assigned to executors\");\n            }\n\n            if (!lockedTasks.contains(taskId)) {\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The task to remove is not locked yet by the task manager\");\n            }\n\n            if (!tasks.containsInitialized(taskId)) {\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The task to remove is not owned by the task manager\");\n            }\n\n            tasks.removeTask(tasks.initializedTask(taskId));\n        });\n\n        log.info(\"Removed task {} from the task manager\", taskId);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public Set<ReadOnlyTask> getTasks() {\n        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> tasks.activeInitializedTasks().stream().map(ReadOnlyTask::new).collect(Collectors.toSet()));\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void setUncaughtException(final StreamsException exception, final TaskId taskId) {\n        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n\n            if (!assignedTasks.containsKey(taskId)) {\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"An uncaught exception can only be set as long as the task is still assigned\");\n            }\n\n            if (uncaughtExceptions.containsKey(taskId)) {\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The uncaught exception must be cleared before restarting processing\");\n            }\n\n            uncaughtExceptions.put(taskId, exception);\n        });\n\n        log.info(\"Set an uncaught exception of type {} for task {}, with error message: {}\",\n            exception.getClass().getName(),\n            taskId,\n            exception.getMessage());\n    }\n\n    public Map<TaskId, RuntimeException> drainUncaughtExceptions() {\n        final Map<TaskId, RuntimeException> returnValue = returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n            final Map<TaskId, RuntimeException> result = new HashMap<>(uncaughtExceptions);\n            uncaughtExceptions.clear();\n            return result;\n        });\n\n        if (!returnValue.isEmpty()) {\n            log.debug(\"Drained {} uncaught exceptions\", returnValue.size());\n        }\n\n        return returnValue;\n    }\n\n    public boolean hasUncaughtException(final TaskId taskId) {\n        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> uncaughtExceptions.containsKey(taskId));\n    }\n\n    private void executeWithTasksLocked(final Runnable action) {\n        tasksLock.lock();\n        try {\n            action.run();\n        } finally {\n            tasksLock.unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    private <T> T returnWithTasksLocked(final Supplier<T> action) {\n        tasksLock.lock();\n        try {\n            return action.get();\n        } finally {\n            tasksLock.unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    private boolean canProgress(final StreamTask task, final long nowMs) {\n        return\n            taskExecutionMetadata.canProcessTask(task, nowMs) && task.isProcessable(nowMs) ||\n                taskExecutionMetadata.canPunctuateTask(task) && (task.canPunctuateStreamTime() || task.canPunctuateSystemTime());\n    }\n\n    public void startTaskExecutors() {\n        for (final TaskExecutor t: taskExecutors) {\n            t.start();\n        }\n    }\n\n    public void shutdown(final Duration duration) {\n        for (final TaskExecutor t: taskExecutors) {\n            t.requestShutdown();\n        }\n        signalTaskExecutors();\n        for (final TaskExecutor t: taskExecutors) {\n            t.awaitShutdown(duration);\n        }\n    }\n}\n","numLines":413,"startLine":1,"totalLines":413}}}

java.util.NoSuchElementException;\n    33→import java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicLong;\n    34→\n    35→/**\n    36→ * An in-memory LRU cache store similar to {@link MemoryLRUCache} but byte-based, not\n    37→ * record based\n    38→ */\n    39→public class ThreadCache {\n    40→    private final Logger log;\n    41→    private volatile long maxCacheSizeBytes;\n    42→    private final StreamsMetricsImpl metrics;\n    43→    private final Map<String, NamedCache> caches = new HashMap<>();\n    44→\n    45→    // Invariant: equal to sum of sizeInBytes of caches\n    46→    private final AtomicLong sizeInBytes = new AtomicLong();\n    47→\n    48→    // internal stats\n    49→    private long numPuts = 0;\n    50→    private long numGets = 0;\n    51→    private long numEvicts = 0;\n    52→    private long numFlushes = 0;\n    53→\n    54→    public interface DirtyEntryFlushListener {\n    55→        void apply(final List<DirtyEntry> dirty);\n    56→    }\n    57→\n    58→    public ThreadCache(final LogContext logContext, final long maxCacheSizeBytes, final StreamsMetricsImpl metrics) {\n    59→        this.maxCacheSizeBytes = maxCacheSizeBytes;\n    60→        this.metrics = metrics;\n    61→        this.log = logContext.logger(getClass());\n    62→    }\n    63→\n    64→    public long puts() {\n    65→        return numPuts;\n    66→    }\n    67→\n    68→    public long gets() {\n    69→        return numGets;\n    70→    }\n    71→\n    72→    public long evicts() {\n    73→        return numEvicts;\n    74→    }\n    75→\n    76→    public long flushes() {\n    77→        return numFlushes;\n    78→    }\n    79→\n    80→    public synchronized void resize(final long newCacheSizeBytes) {\n    81→        final boolean shrink = newCacheSizeBytes < maxCacheSizeBytes;\n    82→        maxCacheSizeBytes = newCacheSizeBytes;\n    83→        if (shrink) {\n    84→            log.debug(\"Cache size was shrunk to {}\", newCacheSizeBytes);\n    85→            if (caches.values().isEmpty()) {\n    86→                return;\n    87→            }\n    88→            final CircularIterator<NamedCache> circularIterator = new CircularIterator<>(caches.values());\n    89→            while (sizeInBytes.get() > maxCacheSizeBytes) {\n    90→                final NamedCache cache = circularIterator.next();\n    91→                synchronized (cache) {\n    92→                    final long oldSize = cache.sizeInBytes();\n    93→                    cache.evict();\n    94→                    sizeInBytes.getAndAdd(cache.sizeInBytes() - oldSize);\n    95→                }\n    96→                numEvicts++;\n    97→            }\n    98→        } else {\n    99→            log.debug(\"Cache size was expanded to {}\", newCacheSizeBytes);\n   100→        }\n   101→    }\n   102→\n   103→    /**\n   104→     * The thread cache maintains a set of {@link NamedCache}s whose names are a concatenation of the task ID and the\n   105→     * underlying store name. This method creates those names.\n   106→     * @param taskIDString Task ID\n   107→     * @param underlyingStoreName Underlying store name\n   108→     */\n   109→    public static String nameSpaceFromTaskIdAndStore(final String taskIDString, final String underlyingStoreName) {\n   110→        return taskIDString + \"-\" + underlyingStoreName;\n   111→    }\n   112→\n   113→    /**\n   114→     * Given a cache name of the form taskid-storename, return the task ID.\n   115→     */\n   116→    public static String taskIDfromCacheName(final String cacheName) {\n   117→        final name.\n   123→     */\n   124→    public static String underlyingStoreNamefromCacheName(final String cacheName) {\n   125→        final String[] tokens = cacheName.split(\"-\", 2);\n   126→        return tokens[1];\n   127→    }\n   128→\n   129→\n   130→    /**\n   131→     * Add a listener that is called each time an entry is evicted from the cache or an explicit flush is called\n   132→     */\n   133→    public void addDirtyEntryFlushListener(final String namespace, final DirtyEntryFlushListener listener) {\n   134→        final NamedCache cache = getOrCreateCache(namespace);\n   135→        cache.setListener(listener);\n   136→    }\n   137→\n   138→    public void flush(final String namespace) {\n   139→        numFlushes++;\n   140→\n   141→        final NamedCache cache = getCache(namespace);\n   142→        if (cache == null) {\n   143→            return;\n   144→        }\n   145→\n   146→        synchronized (cache) {\n   147→            final long oldSize = cache.sizeInBytes();\n   148→            cache.flush();\n   149→            sizeInBytes.getAndAdd(cache.sizeInBytes() - oldSize);\n   150→        }\n   151→\n   152→        if (log.isTraceEnabled()) {\n   153→            log.trace(\"Cache stats on flush: #puts={}, #gets={}, #evicts={}, #flushes={}\", puts(), gets(), evicts(), flushes());\n   154→        }\n   155→    }\n   156→\n   157→    public LRUCacheEntry get(final String namespace, final Bytes key) {\n   158→        numGets++;\n   159→\n   160→        if (key == null) {\n   161→            return null;\n   162→        }\n   163→\n   164→        final NamedCache cache = getCache(namespace);\n   165→        if (cache == null) {\n   166→            return null;\n   167→        }\n   168→        return cache.get(key);\n   169→    }\n   170→\n   171→    public void put(final String namespace, final Bytes key, final LRUCacheEntry value) {\n   172→        numPuts++;\n   173→\n   174→        final NamedCache cache = getOrCreateCache(namespace);\n   175→\n   176→        synchronized (cache) {\n   177→            final long oldSize = cache.sizeInBytes();\n   178→            cache.put(key, value);\n   179→            sizeInBytes.getAndAdd(cache.sizeInBytes() - oldSize);\n   180→            maybeEvict(namespace, cache);\n   181→        }\n   182→    }\n   183→\n   184→    public LRUCacheEntry putIfAbsent(final String namespace, final Bytes key, final LRUCacheEntry value) {\n   185→        final NamedCache cache = getOrCreateCache(namespace);\n   186→        final LRUCacheEntry result;\n   187→        synchronized (cache) {\n   188→            final long oldSize = cache.sizeInBytes();\n   189→            result = cache.putIfAbsent(key, value);\n   190→            sizeInBytes.getAndAdd(cache.sizeInBytes() - oldSize);\n   191→            maybeEvict(namespace, cache);\n   192→        }\n   193→\n   194→        if (result == null) {\n   195→            numPuts++;\n   196→        }\n   197→        return result;\n   198→    }\n   199→\n   200→    public void putAll(final String namespace, final List<KeyValue<Bytes, LRUCacheEntry>> entries) {\n   201→        for (final KeyValue<Bytes, LRUCacheEntry> entry : entries) {\n   202→            put(namespace, entry.key, entry.value);\n   203→        }\n   204→    }\n   205→\n   206→    public LRUCacheEntry delete(final String namespace, final Bytes key) {\n   207→        final NamedCache cache = getCache(namespace);\n   208→        if (cache == null) {\n   209→            return null;\n   210→        }\n   211→\n   212→        final LRUCacheEntry entry;\n   213→        synchronized (cache) {\n   214→            final long oldSize = cache.sizeInBytes();\n   215→            entry = cache.delete(key);\n   216→            sizeInBytes.getAndAdd(cache.sizeInBytes() - oldSize);\n   217→        }\n   218→        return entry;\n   219→    }\n   220→\n   221→    public MemoryLRUCacheBytesIterator range(final String namespace, final Bytes from, final Bytes to) {\n   222→        return range(namespace, from, to, true);\n   223→    }\n   224→\n   225→    public MemoryLRUCacheBytesIterator range(final String namespace, final Bytes from, final Bytes to, final boolean toInclusive) {\n   226→        final NamedCache cache = getCache(namespace);\n   227→ */\npublic class ThreadCache {\n    private final Logger log;\n    private volatile long maxCacheSizeBytes;\n    private final StreamsMetricsImpl metrics;\n    private final Map<String, NamedCache> 0;\n    private long numGets = 0;\n    private long numEvicts = 0;\n    private long numFlushes = 0;\n\n    public interface DirtyEntryFlushListener {\n        void apply(final List<DirtyEntry> dirty);\n    }\n\n    public ThreadCache(final LogContext logContext, final long maxCacheSizeBytes, final StreamsMetricsImpl metrics) {\n        this.maxCacheSizeBytes = maxCacheSizeBytes;\n        this.metrics = metrics;\n        this.log = logContext.logger(getClass());\n    }\n\n    public long puts() {\n        return numPuts;\n    }\n\n    public long gets() {\n        return numGets;\n    }\n\n    public long evicts() {\n        return numEvicts;\n    }\n\n    public long flushes() {\n        return numFlushes;\n    }\n\n    public synchronized void resize(final long newCacheSizeBytes) {\n        final boolean shrink = newCacheSizeBytes < maxCacheSizeBytes;\n        maxCacheSizeBytes = newCacheSizeBytes;\n        if (shrink) {\n            log.debug(\"Cache size was shrunk to {}\", newCacheSizeBytes);\n            if (caches.values().isEmpty()) {\n                return;\n            }\n            final CircularIterator<NamedCache> circularIterator = new CircularIterator<>(caches.values());\n            while (sizeInBytes.get() > maxCacheSizeBytes) {\n                final NamedCache cache = circularIterator.next();\n                synchronized (cache) {\n                    final long oldSize = cache.sizeInBytes();\n                    cache.evict();\n                    sizeInBytes.getAndAdd(cache.sizeInBytes() - oldSize);\n                }\n                numEvicts++;\n            }\n        } else {\n            log.debug(\"Cache size was expanded to {}\", newCacheSizeBytes);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * The thread cache This method creates those names.\n     * @param taskIDString Task ID\n     * @param underlyingStoreName Underlying store name\n     */\n    public static String nameSpaceFromTaskIdAndStore(final String taskIDString, final String underlyingStoreName) {\n        return taskIDString + \"-\" + underlyingStoreName;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Given a cache name of the form taskid-storename, return - oldSize);\n        }\n\n        if (log.isTraceEnabled()) {\n            log.trace(\"Cache stats on flush: #puts={}, #gets={}, #evicts={}, #flushes={}\", puts(), gets(), evicts(), flushes());\n        }\n    }\n\n    public LRUCacheEntry get(final String namespace, final Bytes key) {\n        numGets++;\n\n        if (key == null) {\n            return null;\n        }\n\n        final NamedCache cache = getCache(namespace);\n        if (cache == null) {\n            return null;\n        }\n        return cache.get(key);\n    }\n\n    public void put(final String namespace, final Bytes key, final LRUCacheEntry value) {\n        numPuts++;\n\n        final NamedCache cache = getOrCreateCache(namespace);\n\n        synchronized (cache) {\n            final long oldSize = cache.sizeInBytes();\n            cache.put(key, value);\n            sizeInBytes.getAndAdd(cache.sizeInBytes() - oldSize);\n            maybeEvict(namespace, cache);\n        }\n    }\n\n    public LRUCacheEntry putIfAbsent(final String namespace, final Bytes key, final LRUCacheEntry value) {\n        final NamedCache cache = getOrCreateCache(namespace);\n        final LRUCacheEntry result;\n        synchronized (cache) {\n            final long oldSize = cache.sizeInBytes();\n            result = cache.putIfAbsent(key, value);\n            sizeInBytes.getAndAdd(cache.sizeInBytes() - oldSize);\n            maybeEvict(namespace, cache);\n        }\n\n        if (result == null) {\n            numPuts++;\n        }\n        return result;\n    }\n\n    public void putAll(final    }\n    }\n\n    public LRUCacheEntry delete(final String namespace, final Bytes key) {\n        final NamedCache cache = getCache(namespace);\n        if (cache == null) {\n            return    public long size() {\n        long size = 0;\n        for (final NamedCache cache : caches.values()) {\n            size += cache.size();\n            if (isOverflowing(size)) {\n  return sizeInBytes.get();\n    }\n\n    synchronized void close(final String namespace) {\n        final NamedCache removed = caches.remove(namespace);\n        if (removed != null) {\n String namespace, final NamedCache cache) {\n        int numEvicted = 0;\n        while (sizeInBytes.get() > maxCacheSizeBytes) {\n            // we abort here as the put on this cache may have triggered\n            // a put on another cache. So even though the sizeInBytes() is\n            // still > maxCacheSizeBytes there is nothing to evict from this\n            // namespaced cache.\n            if (cache.isEmpty()) {\n NamedCache cache = caches.get(name);\n        if (cache == null) {\n            cache = new NamedCache(name, this.metrics);\n            caches.put(name, cache);\n        }\n        return cache;\n    private KeyValue<Bytes, LRUCacheEntry> nextEntry;\n\n        MemoryLRUCacheBytesIterator(final Iterator<Bytes> keys, final NamedCache cache) {\n            this.keys = keys;\n            this.cache   }\n\n\n        public KeyValue<Bytes, LRUCacheEntry> peekNext() {\n            if (!hasNext()) {\n                throw new NoSuchElementException();\n            }\n            return nextEntry;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public boolean hasNext() {\n            if (nextEntry != null) {\n                return true;\n            }\n\n            while (keys.hasNext() && nextEntry == null) {\n                internalNext();\n            }\n\n            return nextEntry != null;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public KeyValue<Bytes, LRUCacheEntry> next() {\n            if (!hasNext()) {\n                throw new NoSuchElementException();\n            }\n            final KeyValue<Bytes, LRUCacheEntry> result = nextEntry;\n            nextEntry = null;\n            return result;\n        }\n\n        private void internalNext() {\n            final Bytes cacheKey = keys.next();\n            final LRUCacheEntry entry = cache.get(cacheKey);\n            if (entry == null) {\n                return;\n            }\n\n            nextEntry = new KeyValue<>(cacheKey, entry);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void close() {\n            // do nothing\n        }\n    }\n\n    static class DirtyEntry {\n        private final Bytes key;\n        private final byte[] newValue;\n        private final LRUCacheEntry recordContext;\n\n
