threadVersions.put(threadName, 0L);\n    }\n\n    public void unregisterThread(final String threadName) {\n        threadVersions.remove(threadName);\n        maybeNotifyTopologyVersionListeners();\n    }\n\n    public TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata() {\n        return taskExecutionMetadata;\n    }\n\n    public void executeTopologyUpdatesAndBumpThreadVersion(final Consumer<Set<String>> handleTopologyAdditions,\n                                                           final Consumer<Set<String>> handleTopologyRemovals) {\n        try {\n            version.topologyLock.lock();\n            final long latestTopologyVersion = topologyVersion();\n            handleTopologyAdditions.accept(namedTopologiesView());\n            handleTopologyRemovals.accept(namedTopologiesView());\n            threadVersions.put(Thread.currentThread().getName(), latestTopologyVersion);\n        } finally {\n            version.topologyLock.unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    public void maybeNotifyTopologyVersionListeners() {\n        try {\n            lock();\n            final long minThreadVersion = minimumThreadVersion();\n            final Iterator<TopologyVersionListener> iterator = version.activeTopologyUpdateListeners.listIterator();\n            TopologyVersionListener topologyVersionListener;\n            while (iterator.hasNext()) {\n                topologyVersionListener = iterator.next();\n                final long topologyVersionWaitersVersion = topologyVersionListener.topologyVersion;\n                if (minThreadVersion >= topologyVersionWaitersVersion) {\n                    topologyVersionListener.future.complete(null);\n                    iterator.remove();\n                    log.info(\"All threads are now on topology version {}\", topologyVersionListener.topologyVersion);\n                }\n            }\n        } finally {\n            unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Return the minimum version across all live threads, or Long.MAX_VALUE if there are no threads running\n    private long minimumThreadVersion() {\n        final Optional<Long> minVersion = threadVersions.values().stream().min(Long::compare);\n        return minVersion.orElse(Long.MAX_VALUE);\n    }\n\n    public void wakeupThreads() {\n        try {\n            lock();\n            version.topologyCV.signalAll();\n        } finally {\n            unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    public void registerLifecycleObserver(final TopologyLifecycleObserver observer) {\n        lock();\n        try {\n            lifecycleObservers.add(observer);\n        } finally {\n            unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void notifyLifecycleObservers() {\n        final long currentVersion = version.topologyVersion.get();\n        final Set<String> activeTopologies = namedTopologiesView();\n        for (final TopologyLifecycleObserver observer : lifecycleObservers) {\n            try {\n                observer.onTopologyChange(currentVersion, activeTopologies);\n            } catch (final Exception e) {\n                log.warn(\"Topology lifecycle observer threw exception\", e);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    public boolean isTaskTopologyStale(final TaskId taskId) {\n        lock();\n        try {\n            final String topologyName = taskId.topologyName();\n            if (topologyName != null) {\n                return !builders.containsKey(topologyName);\n            }\n            return false;\n        } finally {\n            unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    public void maybeWaitForNonEmptyTopology(final Supplier<StreamThread.State> threadState) {\n        if (isEmpty() && threadState.get().isAlive()) {\n            try {\n                lock();\n                while (isEmpty() && threadState.get().isAlive()) {\n                    try {\n                        log.debug(\"Detected that the topology is currently empty, waiting for something to process\");\n                        version.topologyCV.await();\n                    } catch (final InterruptedException e) {\n                        log.error(\"StreamThread was interrupted while waiting on empty topology\", e);\n                    }\n                }\n            } finally {\n                unlock();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Adds the topology and registers a future that listens for all threads on the older version to see the update\n     */\n    public void registerAndBuildNewTopology(final KafkaFutureImpl<Void> future, final InternalTopologyBuilder newTopologyBuilder) {\n        try {\n            lock();\n            buildAndVerifyTopology(newTopologyBuilder);\n            log.info(\"New NamedTopology {} passed validation and will be added, old topology version is {}\", newTopologyBuilder.topologyName(), version.topologyVersion.get());\n            version.topologyVersion.incrementAndGet();\n            version.activeTopologyUpdateListeners.add(new TopologyVersionListener(topologyVersion(), future));\n            builders.put(newTopologyBuilder.topologyName(), newTopologyBuilder);\n            wakeupThreads();\n            notifyLifecycleObservers();\n            log.info(\"Added NamedTopology {} and updated topology version to {}\", newTopologyBuilder.topologyName(), version.topologyVersion.get());\n        } catch (final Throwable throwable) {\n            log.error(\"Failed to add NamedTopology {}, please retry the operation.\", newTopologyBuilder.topologyName());\n            future.completeExceptionally(throwable);\n        } finally {\n            unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Pauses a topology by name\n     * @param topologyName Name of the topology to pause\n     */\n    public void pauseTopology(final String topologyName) {\n        pausedTopologies.add(topologyName);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Checks if a given topology is paused.\n     * @param topologyName If null, assume that we are checking the `UNNAMED_TOPOLOGY`.\n     * @return A boolean indicating if the topology is paused.\n     */\n    public boolean isPaused(final String topologyName) {\n        return pausedTopologies.contains(getTopologyNameOrElseUnnamed(topologyName));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Resumes a topology by name\n     * @param topologyName Name of the topology to resume\n     */\n    public void resumeTopology(final String topologyName) {\n        pausedTopologies.remove(topologyName);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Removes the topology and registers a future that listens for all threads on the older version to see the update\n     */\n    public KafkaFuture<Void> unregisterTopology(final KafkaFutureImpl<Void> removeTopologyFuture,\n                                                final String topologyName) {\n        try {\n            lock();\n            log.info(\"Beginning removal of NamedTopology {}, old topology version is {}\", topologyName, version.topologyVersion.get());\n            version.topologyVersion.incrementAndGet();\n            version.activeTopologyUpdateListeners.add(new TopologyVersionListener(topologyVersion(), removeTopologyFuture));\n            final InternalTopologyBuilder removedBuilder = builders.remove(topologyName);\n            removedBuilder.fullSourceTopicNames().forEach(allInputTopics::remove);\n            removedBuilder.allSourcePatternStrings().forEach(allInputTopics::remove);\n            notifyLifecycleObservers();\n            log.info(\"Finished removing NamedTopology {}, topology version was updated to {}\", topologyName, version.topologyVersion.get());\n        } catch (final Throwable throwable) {\n            log.error(\"Failed to remove NamedTopology {}, please retry.\", topologyName);\n            removeTopologyFuture.completeExceptionally(throwable);\n        } finally {\n            unlock();\n        }\n        return removeTopologyFuture;\n    }\n\n    public TaskConfig taskConfig(final TaskId taskId) {\n        final InternalTopologyBuilder builder = lookupBuilderForTask(taskId);\n        return builder.topologyConfigs().getTaskConfig();\n    }\n\n    public void buildAndRewriteTopology() {\n        applyToEachBuilder(this::buildAndVerifyTopology);\n    }\n\n    private void buildAndVerifyTopology(final InternalTopologyBuilder builder) {\n        builder.rewriteTopology(config);\n        builder.buildTopology();\n\n        final Set<String> allInputTopicsCopy = new HashSet<>(allInputTopics);\n\n        // As we go, check each topology for overlap in the set of input topics/patterns\n        final int numInputTopics = allInputTopicsCopy.size();\n        final List<String> inputTopics = builder.fullSourceTopicNames();\n        final Collection<String> inputPatterns = builder.allSourcePatternStrings();\n\n        final Set<String> newInputTopics = new HashSet<>(inputTopics);\n        newInputTopics.addAll(inputPatterns);\n\n        final int numNewInputTopics = newInputTopics.size();\n        allInputTopicsCopy.addAll(newInputTopics);\n\n        if (allInputTopicsCopy.size() != numInputTopics + numNewInputTopics) {\n            inputTopics.retainAll(allInputTopicsCopy);\n            inputPatterns.retainAll(allInputTopicsCopy);\n            log.error(\"Tried to add the NamedTopology {} but it had overlap with other input topics {} or patterns {}\",\n                      builder.topologyName(), inputTopics, inputPatterns);\n            throw new TopologyException(\"Named Topologies may not subscribe to the same input topics or patterns\");\n        }\n\n        final ProcessorTopology globalTopology = builder.buildGlobalStateTopology();\n        if (globalTopology != null) {\n            if (builder.topologyName() != null) {\n                throw new IllegalStateException(\"Global state stores are not supported with Named Topologies\");\n            } else if (this.globalTopology != null) {\n                throw new TopologyException(\"Topology builder had global state, but global topology has already been set\");\n            } else {\n                this.globalTopology = globalTopology;\n                globalStateStores.putAll(builder.globalStateStores());\n            }\n        }\n        allInputTopics.addAll(newInputTopics);\n    }\n\n    public int numStreamThreads(final StreamsConfig config) {\n        final int configuredNumStreamThreads = config.getInt(StreamsConfig.NUM_STREAM_THREADS_CONFIG);\n\n        // If there are named topologies but some are empty, this indicates a bug in user code\n        if (hasNamedTopologies()) {\n            if (hasNoLocalTopology()) {\n                log.error(\"Detected a named topology with no input topics, a named topology may not be empty.\");\n                throw new TopologyException(\"Topology has no stream threads and no global threads, \" +\n                                                \"must subscribe to at least one source topic or pattern.\");\n            }\n        } else {\n            // If both the global and non-global topologies are empty, this indicates a bug in user code\n            if (hasNoLocalTopology() && !hasGlobalTopology()) {\n                log.error(\"Topology with no input topics will create no stream threads and no global thread.\");\n                throw new TopologyException(\"Topology has no stream threads and no global threads, \" +\n                                                \"must subscribe to at least one source topic or global table.\");\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Lastly we check for an empty non-global topology and override the threads to zero if set otherwise\n        if (configuredNumStreamThreads != 0 && hasNoLocalTopology()) {\n            log.info(\"Overriding number of StreamThreads to zero for global-only topology\");\n            return 0;\n        }\n\n        return configuredNumStreamThreads;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @return true iff the app is using named topologies, or was started up with no topology at all\n     */\n    public boolean hasNamedTopologies() {\n        return !builders.containsKey(UNNAMED_TOPOLOGY);\n    }\n\n    public Set<String> namedTopologiesView() {\n        return hasNamedTopologies() ? Collections.unmodifiableSet(builders.keySet()) : emptySet();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @return true iff any of the topologies have a global topology\n     */\n    public boolean hasGlobalTopology() {\n        return evaluateConditionIsTrueForAnyBuilders(InternalTopologyBuilder::hasGlobalStores);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @return true iff any of the topologies have no local (aka non-global) topology\n     */\n    public boolean hasNoLocalTopology() {\n        return evaluateConditionIsTrueForAnyBuilders(InternalTopologyBuilder::hasNoLocalTopology);\n    }\n\n    public boolean hasPersistentStores() {\n        // If the app is using named topologies, there may not be any persistent state when it first starts up\n        // but a new NamedTopology may introduce it later, so we must return true\n        if (hasNamedTopologies()) {\n            return true;\n        }\n        return evaluateConditionIsTrueForAnyBuilders(InternalTopologyBuilder::hasPersistentStores);\n    }\n\n    public boolean hasStore(final String name) {\n        return evaluateConditionIsTrueForAnyBuilders(b -> b.hasStore(name));\n    }\n\n    public boolean hasOffsetResetOverrides() {\n        // Return true if using named topologies, as there may be named topologies added later which do have overrides\n        return hasNamedTopologies() || evaluateConditionIsTrueForAnyBuilders(InternalTopologyBuilder::hasOffsetResetOverrides);\n    }\n\n    public Optional<AutoOffsetResetStrategy> offsetResetStrategy(final String topic) {\n        for (final InternalTopologyBuilder builder : builders.values()) {\n            if (builder.containsTopic(topic)) {\n                return Optional.ofNullable(builder.offsetResetStrategy(topic));\n            }\n        }\n        log.warn(\"Unable to look up offset reset strategy for topic {} \" +\n            \"as this topic does not appear in the sources of any of the current topologies: {}\\n \" +\n                \"This may be due to natural race condition when removing a topology but it should not \" +\n                \"persist or appear frequently.\",\n            topic, namedTopologiesView()\n        );\n        // returning `null` for an Optional return type triggers spotbugs\n        // we added an exception for NP_OPTIONAL_RETURN_NULL for this method\n        // when we remove NamedTopologies, we can remove this exception\n        return null;\n    }\n\n\n    public Collection<String> fullSourceTopicNamesForTopology(final String topologyName) {\n        Objects.requireNonNull(topologyName, \"topology name must not be null\");\n        return lookupBuilderForNamedTopology(topologyName).fullSourceTopicNames();\n    }\n\n    public Collection<String> allFullSourceTopicNames() {\n        final List<String> sourceTopics = new ArrayList<>();\n        applyToEachBuilder(b -> sourceTopics.addAll(b.fullSourceTopicNames()));\n        return sourceTopics;\n    }\n\n    Pattern sourceTopicPattern() {\n        final StringBuilder patternBuilder = new StringBuilder();\n\n        applyToEachBuilder(b -> {\n            final String patternString = b.sourceTopicPatternString();\n            if (!patternString.isEmpty()) {\n                patternBuilder.append(patternString).append(\"|\");\n            }\n        });\n\n        if (patternBuilder.length() > 0) {\n            patternBuilder.setLength(patternBuilder.length() - 1);\n            return Pattern.compile(patternBuilder.toString());\n        } else {\n            return EMPTY_ZERO_LENGTH_PATTERN;\n        }\n    }\n\n    public boolean usesPatternSubscription() {\n        return evaluateConditionIsTrueForAnyBuilders(InternalTopologyBuilder::usesPatternSubscription);\n    }\n\n    // Can be empty if app is started up with no Named Topologies, in order to add them on later\n    public boolean isEmpty() {\n        return builders.isEmpty();\n    }\n\n    public String topologyDescriptionString() {\n        if (isEmpty()) {\n            return \"\";\n        }\n        final StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();\n\n        applyToEachBuilder(b -> sb.append(b.describe().toString()));\n\n        return sb.toString();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @return the {@link   */\n    public ProcessorTopology buildSubtopology(final TaskId task) {\n        final InternalTopologyBuilder builder = lookupBuilderForTask(task);\n        return state stores are not supported with Named Topologies\");\n        }\n        return globalTopology;\n    }\n\n    public Map<String, StateStore> globalStateStores() {\n        return Logger log;\n\n    // the \"__\" (double underscore) string is not allowed for topology names, so it's safe to use to indicate\n    // that it's not a named topology\n    public static final String ProcessingMode processingMode;\n    private final TopologyVersion version;\n    private final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata;\n    private final Set<String> pausedTopologies;\n\n HashSet<>();\n    private final Map<String, Long> threadVersions = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();\n\n    public static class TopologyVersion {\n        public AtomicLong topologyVersion = new AtomicLong(0L); // the local topology version\n        public ReentrantLock topologyLock = new ReentrantLock();\n        public Condition topologyCV = topologyLock.newCondition();\n        public List<TopologyVersionListener> activeTopologyUpdateListeners = new LinkedList<>();\n    }\n\n    public static class TopologyVersionListener {\n        final long topologyVersion; // the (minimum) version to wait for these threads to cross\n        final KafkaFutureImpl<Void> future; // the future waiting on all threads to be updated\n\n        public TopologyVersionListener(final long topologyVersion, final KafkaFutureImpl<Void> future) {\n            this.topologyVersion = topologyVersion;\n            this.future = future;\n        }\n    }\n\n    public interface TopologyLifecycleObserver {\n        void onTopologyChange(long newVersion, Set<String> activeTopologies);\n    }\n\n    public TopologyMetadata(final InternalTopologyBuilder builder,\n                            final StreamsConfig config) {\n        this.version = new TopologyVersion();\n        this.processingMode = StreamsConfigUtils.processingMode(config);\n        this.config = config;\n        this.log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(getClass());\n        this.pausedTopologies = ConcurrentHashMap.newKeySet();\n\n        builders = new ConcurrentSkipListMap<>();\n        if (builder.hasNamedTopology()) {\n            builders.put(builder.topologyName(), builder);\n        } else {\n            builders.put(UNNAMED_TOPOLOGY, builder);\n        }\n        this.taskExecutionMetadata = new TaskExecutionMetadata(builders.keySet(), pausedTopologies, processingMode);\n    }\n\n    public TopologyMetadata(final ConcurrentNavigableMap<String, InternalTopologyBuilder> builders,\n                            final StreamsConfig config) {\n        this.version = new TopologyVersion();\n        this.processingMode = StreamsConfigUtils.processingMode(config);\n        this.config = config;\n        this.log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(getClass());\n        this.pausedTopologies = ConcurrentHashMap.newKeySet();\n\n        this.builders = builders;\n        if (builders.isEmpty()) {\n            log.info(\"Created an empty KafkaStreams app with no topology\");\n        }\n        this.taskExecutionMetadata = new TaskExecutionMetadata(builders.keySet(), pausedTopologies, processingMode);\n    }\n\n    // Need to (re)set the log here to pick up the `processId` part of the clientId in the prefix\n    public void setLog(final LogContext logContext) {\n        log = logContext.logger(getClass());\n    }\n    \n    public ProcessingMode processingMode() {\n        return processingMode;\n    }\n\n    public long topologyVersion() {\n        return version.topologyVersion.get();\n    }\n\n    private void lock() {\n        version.topologyLock.lock();\n    }\n\n    private void unlock() {\n        version.topologyLock.unlock();\n    }\n\n    public Collection<String> sourceTopicsForTopology(final String name) {\n        return builders.get(name).fullSourceTopicNames();\n    }\n\n    public boolean needsUpdate(final String threadName) {\n        return threadVersions.get(threadName) < topologyVersion();\n    }\n\n    public void registerThread(final String threadName) {\n        threadVersions.put(threadName, 0L);\n    }\n\n    public void unregisterThread(final String threadName) {\n        threadVersions.remove(threadName);\n        maybeNotifyTopologyVersionListeners();\n    }\n\n    public TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata() {\n        return taskExecutionMetadata;\n    }\n\n    public void executeTopologyUpdatesAndBumpThreadVersion(final Consumer<Set<String>> handleTopologyAdditions,\n                                                           final Consumer<Set<String>> handleTopologyRemovals) {\n        try {\n            version.topologyLock.lock();\n            final long latestTopologyVersion = topologyVersion();\n            handleTopologyAdditions.accept(namedTopologiesView());\n            handleTopologyRemovals.accept(namedTopologiesView());\n            threadVersions.put(Thread.currentThread().getName(), latestTopologyVersion);\n        } finally {\n            version.topologyLock.unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    public void maybeNotifyTopologyVersionListeners() {\n        try {\n            lock();\n            final long minThreadVersion = minimumThreadVersion();\n            final Iterator<TopologyVersionListener> iterator = version.activeTopologyUpdateListeners.listIterator();\n            TopologyVersionListener topologyVersionListener;\n            while (iterator.hasNext()) {\n                topologyVersionListener = iterator.next();\n                final long topologyVersionWaitersVersion = topologyVersionListener.topologyVersion;\n                if (minThreadVersion >= topologyVersionWaitersVersion) {\n                    topologyVersionListener.future.complete(null);\n                    iterator.remove();\n                    log.info(\"All threads are now on topology version {}\", topologyVersionListener.topologyVersion);\n                }\n            }\n        } finally {\n            unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Return the minimum version across all live threads, or Long.MAX_VALUE if there are no threads running\n    private long minimumThreadVersion() {\n        final Optional<Long> minVersion = threadVersions.values().stream().min(Long::compare);\n        return minVersion.orElse(Long.MAX_VALUE);\n    }\n\n    public void wakeupThreads() {\n        try {\n            lock();\n            version.topologyCV.signalAll();\n        } finally {\n            unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    public void registerLifecycleObserver(final TopologyLifecycleObserver observer) {\n        lock();\n        try {\n            lifecycleObservers.add(observer);\n        } finally {\n            unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void notifyLifecycleObservers() {\n        final long currentVersion = version.topologyVersion.get();\n        final Set<String> activeTopologies = namedTopologiesView();\n        for (final TopologyLifecycleObserver observer : lifecycleObservers) {\n            try {\n                observer.onTopologyChange(currentVersion, activeTopologies);\n            } catch (final Exception e) {\n                log.warn(\"Topology lifecycle observer threw exception\", e);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    public boolean isTaskTopologyStale(final TaskId taskId) {\n        lock();\n        try {\n            final String topologyName = taskId.topologyName();\n            if (topologyName != null) {\n                return !builders.containsKey(topologyName);\n            }\n            return false;\n        } finally {\n            unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    public void maybeWaitForNonEmptyTopology(final Supplier<StreamThread.State> threadState) {\n        if (isEmpty() && threadState.get().isAlive()) {\n            try {\n                lock();\n                while (isEmpty() && threadState.get().isAlive()) {\n                    try {\n                        log.debug(\"Detected that the topology is currently empty, waiting for something to process\");\n                        version.topologyCV.await();\n                    } catch (final InterruptedException e) {\n                        log.error(\"StreamThread was interrupted while waiting on empty topology\", e);\n                    }\n                }\n            } finally {\n                unlock();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Adds the topology and registers a future that listens for all threads on the older version to see the update\n     */\n    public void registerAndBuildNewTopology(final KafkaFutureImpl<Void> future, final InternalTopologyBuilder newTopologyBuilder) {\n        boolean shouldNotifyObservers = false;\n        try {\n            lock();\n            buildAndVerifyTopology(newTopologyBuilder);\n            log.info(\"New NamedTopology {} passed validation and will be added, old topology version is {}\", newTopologyBuilder.topologyName(), version.topologyVersion.get());\n            version.topologyVersion.incrementAndGet();\n            version.activeTopologyUpdateListeners.add(new TopologyVersionListener(topologyVersion(), future));\n            builders.put(newTopologyBuilder.topologyName(), newTopologyBuilder);\n            wakeupThreads();\n            shouldNotifyObservers = true;\n            log.info(\"Added NamedTopology {} and updated topology version to {}\", newTopologyBuilder.topologyName(), version.topologyVersion.get());\n        } catch (final Throwable throwable) {\n            log.error(\"Failed to add NamedTopology {}, please retry the operation.\", newTopologyBuilder.topologyName());\n            future.completeExceptionally(throwable);\n        } finally {\n            unlock();\n        }\n        if (shouldNotifyObservers) {\n            notifyLifecycleObservers();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Pauses a topology by name\n     * @param topologyName Name of the topology to pause\n     */\n    public void pauseTopology(final String topologyName) {\n        pausedTopologies.add(topologyName);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Checks if a given topology is paused.\n     * @param topologyName If null, assume that we are checking the `UNNAMED_TOPOLOGY`.\n     * @return A boolean indicating if the topology is paused.\n     */\n    public boolean isPaused(final String topologyName) {\n        return pausedTopologies.contains(getTopologyNameOrElseUnnamed(topologyName));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Resumes a topology by name\n     * @param topologyName Name of the topology to resume\n     */\n    public void resumeTopology(final String topologyName) {\n        pausedTopologies.remove(topologyName);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Removes the topology and registers a future that listens for all threads on the older version to see the update\n     */\n    public KafkaFuture<Void> unregisterTopology(final KafkaFutureImpl<Void> removeTopologyFuture,\n                                                final String topologyName) {\n        try {\n            lock();\n            log.info(\"Beginning removal of NamedTopology {}, old topology version is {}\", topologyName, version.topologyVersion.get());\n            version.topologyVersion.incrementAndGet();\n            version.activeTopologyUpdateListeners.add(new TopologyVersionListener(topologyVersion(), removeTopologyFuture));\n            final InternalTopologyBuilder removedBuilder = builders.remove(topologyName);\n            removedBuilder.fullSourceTopicNames().forEach(allInputTopics::remove);\n            removedBuilder.allSourcePatternStrings().forEach(allInputTopics::remove);\n            notifyLifecycleObservers();\n            log.info(\"Finished removing NamedTopology {}, topology version was updated to {}\", topologyName, version.topologyVersion.get());\n        } catch (final Throwable throwable) {\n            log.error(\"Failed to remove NamedTopology {}, please retry.\", topologyName);\n            removeTopologyFuture.completeExceptionally(throwable);\n        } finally {\n            unlock();\n        }\n        return removeTopologyFuture;\n    }\n\n    public TaskConfig taskConfig(final TaskId taskId) {\n        final InternalTopologyBuilder builder = lookupBuilderForTask(taskId);\n        return builder.topologyConfigs().getTaskConfig();\n    }\n\n    public void buildAndRewriteTopology() {\n        applyToEachBuilder(this::buildAndVerifyTopology);\n    }\n\n    private void buildAndVerifyTopology(final InternalTopologyBuilder builder) {\n        builder.rewriteTopology(config);\n        builder.buildTopology();\n\n        final Set<String> allInputTopicsCopy = new HashSet<>(allInputTopics);\n\n        // As we go, check each topology for overlap in the set of input topics/patterns\n        final int numInputTopics = allInputTopicsCopy.size();\n        final List<String> inputTopics = builder.fullSourceTopicNames();\n        final Collection<String> inputPatterns = builder.allSourcePatternStrings();\n\n        final Set<String> newInputTopics = new HashSet<>(inputTopics);\n        newInputTopics.addAll(inputPatterns);\n\n        final int numNewInputTopics = newInputTopics.size();\n        allInputTopicsCopy.addAll(newInputTopics);\n\n        if (allInputTopicsCopy.size() != numInputTopics + numNewInputTopics) {\n            inputTopics.retainAll(allInputTopicsCopy);\n            inputPatterns.retainAll(allInputTopicsCopy);\n            log.error(\"Tried to add the NamedTopology {} but it had overlap with other input topics {} or patterns {}\",\n                      builder.topologyName(), inputTopics, inputPatterns);\n            throw new TopologyException(\"Named Topologies may not subscribe to the same input topics or patterns\");\n        }\n\n        final ProcessorTopology globalTopology = builder.buildGlobalStateTopology();\n        if (globalTopology != null) {\n            if (builder.topologyName() != null) {\n                throw new IllegalStateException(\"Global state stores are not supported with Named Topologies\");\n            } else if (this.globalTopology != null) {\n                throw new TopologyException(\"Topology builder had global state, but global topology has already been set\");\n            } else {\n                this.globalTopology = globalTopology;\n                globalStateStores.putAll(builder.globalStateStores());\n            }\n        }\n        allInputTopics.addAll(newInputTopics);\n    }\n\n    public int numStreamThreads(final StreamsConfig config) {\n        final int configuredNumStreamThreads = config.getInt(StreamsConfig.NUM_STREAM_THREADS_CONFIG);\n\n        // If there are named topologies but some are empty, this indicates a bug in user code\n        if (hasNamedTopologies()) {\n            if (hasNoLocalTopology()) {\n                log.error(\"Detected a named topology with no input topics, a named topology may not be empty.\");\n                throw new TopologyException(\"Topology has no stream threads and no global threads, \" +\n                                                \"must subscribe to at least one source topic or pattern.\");\n            }\n        } else {\n            // If both the global and non-global topologies are empty, this indicates a bug in user code\n            if (hasNoLocalTopology() && !hasGlobalTopology()) {\n                log.error(\"Topology with no input topics will create no stream threads and no global thread.\");\n                throw new TopologyException(\"Topology has no stream threads and no global threads, \" +\n                                                \"must subscribe to at least one source topic or global table.\");\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Lastly we check for an empty non-global topology and override the threads to zero if set otherwise\n        if (configuredNumStreamThreads != 0 && hasNoLocalTopology()) {\n            log.info(\"Overriding number of StreamThreads to zero for global-only topology\");\n            return 0;\n        }\n\n        return configuredNumStreamThreads;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @return true iff the app is using named topologies, or was started up with no topology at all\n     */\n    public boolean hasNamedTopologies() {\n        return !builders.containsKey(UNNAMED_TOPOLOGY);\n    }\n\n    public Set<String> namedTopologiesView() {\n        return hasNamedTopologies() ? Collections.unmodifiableSet(builders.keySet()) : emptySet();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @return true iff any of the topologies have a global topology\n     */\n    public boolean hasGlobalTopology() {\n        return evaluateConditionIsTrueForAnyBuilders(InternalTopologyBuilder::hasGlobalStores);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @return true iff any of the topologies have no local (aka non-global) topology\n     */\n    public boolean hasNoLocalTopology() {\n        return evaluateConditionIsTrueForAnyBuilders(InternalTopologyBuilder::hasNoLocalTopology);\n    }\n\n    public boolean hasPersistentStores() {\n        // If the app is using named topologies, there may not be any persistent state when it first starts up\n        // but a new NamedTopology may introduce it later, so we must return true\n        if (hasNamedTopologies()) {\n            return true;\n        }\n        return evaluateConditionIsTrueForAnyBuilders(InternalTopologyBuilder::hasPersistentStores);\n    }\n\n    public boolean hasStore(final String name) {\n        return evaluateConditionIsTrueForAnyBuilders(b -> b.hasStore(name));\n    }\n\n    public boolean hasOffsetResetOverrides() {\n        // Return true if using named topologies, as there may be named topologies added later which do have overrides\n        return hasNamedTopologies() || evaluateConditionIsTrueForAnyBuilders(InternalTopologyBuilder::hasOffsetResetOverrides);\n    }\n\n    public Optional<AutoOffsetResetStrategy> offsetResetStrategy(final String topic) {\n        for (final InternalTopologyBuilder builder : builders.values()) {\n            if (builder.containsTopic(topic)) {\n                return Optional.ofNullable(builder.offsetResetStrategy(topic));\n            }\n        }\n        log.warn(\"Unable to look up offset reset strategy for topic {} \" +\n            \"as this topic does not appear in the sources of any of the current topologies: {}\\n \" +\n                \"This may be due to natural race condition when removing a topology but it should not \" +\n                \"persist or appear frequently.\",\n            topic, when we remove NamedTopologies, we can remove this exception\n        return null;\n    }\n\n\n    public Collection<String> fullSourceTopicNamesForTopology(final String topologyName) {\n sourceTopics;\n    }\n\n    Pattern sourceTopicPattern() {\n        final StringBuilder patternBuilder = new StringBuilder();\n\n        applyToEachBuilder(b -> {\n            final String patternString = b.sourceTopicPatternString();\n            if (!patternString.isEmpty()) {\n                patternBuilder.append(patternString).append(\"|\");\n            }\n        });\n\n 40→ * All tasks contained by the Streams instance.\n    41→ *\n    42→ * Note that these tasks are shared between the TaskManager (stream thread) and the StateUpdater (restore thread),\n    43→ * i.e. been assigned for a NamedTopology that is not yet known by this host. When that occurs we stash\n    55→    // these unknown tasks until either the corresponding NamedTopology is added and we can create them at last, or\n    56→    // we receive a new assignment and Tasks(final LogContext logContext) {\n    66→        this.log = logContext.logger(getClass());\n    67→    }\n    68→\n    69→    @Override\n    70→    public void clearPendingTasksToCreate() {\n    71→        pendingActiveTasksToCreate.clear();\n    72→        pendingStandbyTasksToCreate.clear();\n    73→    }\n    74→\n    75→    @Override\n    76→    public Map<TaskId, Set<TopicPartition>> drainPendingActiveTasksForTopologies(final Set<String> currentTopologies) {\n    77→        final Map<TaskId, Set<TopicPartition>> pendingActiveTasksForTopologies =\n    78→            filterMap(pendingActiveTasksToCreate, t -> currentTopologies.contains(t.getKey().topologyName()));\n    79→\n    80→        pendingActiveTasksToCreate.keySet().removeAll(pendingActiveTasksForTopologies.keySet());\n    81→\n    82→        return pendingActiveTasksForTopologies;\n    83→    }\n    84→\n    85→    @Override\n    86→    public Map<TaskId, Set<TopicPartition>> drainPendingStandbyTasksForTopologies(final Set<String> currentTopologies) {\n    87→        final Map<TaskId, Set<TopicPartition>> org.mockito.Mockito.withSettings;\n\n/**\n * Fray test to verify no deadlock between TopologyMetadata (topologyLock) and\n * DefaultTaskManager (tasksLock).\n *\n * The deadlock scenario (before fix):\n *   Thread A: tasksLock -> topologyLock (via assignNextTask -> isTaskTopologyStale)\n *   Thread B: topologyLock -> tasksLock (via unregisterTopology -> notifyLifecycleObservers -> validateTopologyConsistency)\n *\n * Run: fray-gradle org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.tasks.DeadlockFrayTest -- --iter 1000\n */\npublic class DeadlockFrayTest {\n\n    public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception {\n        final Time time = new MockTime(1L);\n        final StreamsConfig config = new DummyStreamsConfig();\n\n        // Create two named topology builders\n        final InternalTopologyBuilder builder1 = new InternalTopologyBuilder(\n            new TopologyConfig(\"topology1\", config, new Properties()));\n        final InternalTopologyBuilder builder2 = new InternalTopologyBuilder(\n            new TopologyConfig(\"topology2\", config, new Properties()));\n\n        // Build a TopologyMetadata with both named topologies\n        final ConcurrentNavigableMap<String, InternalTopologyBuilder> builders = new ConcurrentSkipListMap<>();\n        builders.put(\"topology1\", builder1);\n        builders.put(\"topology2\", builder2);\n        final TopologyMetadata topologyMetadata = new TopologyMetadata(builders, config);\n\n        // Create mock task with topology name \"topology1\"\n        final TaskId taskId = new TaskId(0, 0, \"topology1\");\n        final StreamTask task = mock(StreamTask.class, withSettings().strictness(LENIENT));\n        when(task.id()).thenReturn(taskId);\n        when(task.isActive()).thenReturn(true);\n        when(task.isProcessable(anyLong())).thenReturn(true);\n        when(task.changelogPartitions()).thenReturn(Collections.emptySet());\n\n        // Create mock TasksRegistry\n        final TasksRegistry tasks = mock(TasksRegistry.class, withSettings().strictness(LENIENT));\n        final Collection<StreamTask> taskCollection = Collections.singleton(task);\n        when(tasks.activeInitializedTasks()).thenReturn(taskCollection);\n        when(tasks.initializedTask(taskId)).thenReturn(task);\n\n        // Create mock TaskExecutor\n        final TaskExecutor executor = mock(TaskExecutor.class, withSettings().strictness(LENIENT));\n        when(executor.name()).thenReturn(\"test-executor\");\n\n        // Create mock TaskExecutionMetadata\n        final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata = mock(TaskExecutionMetadata.class, withSettings().strictness(LENIENT));\n        when(taskExecutionMetadata.canProcessTask(task, 0L)).thenReturn(true);\n\n        // Create DefaultTaskManager - this registers the lifecycle observer on topologyMetadata\n        final DefaultTaskManager taskManager = new DefaultTaskManager(\n            time, \"test-client\", tasks,\n            (tm, name, t, tem) -> executor,\n            taskExecutionMetadata, 1, topologyMetadata\n        );\n\n        // Thread A: assignNextTask acquires tasksLock, then calls isTaskTopologyStale which acquires topologyLock\n        // Thread B: unregisterTopology acquires topologyLock, then (before fix) notifyLifecycleObservers acquires tasksLock\n        final Thread threadA = new Thread(() -> {\n            taskManager.assignNextTask(executor);\n        }, \"thread-assign\");\n\n        final Thread threadB = new Thread(() -> {\n            try {\n                topologyMetadata.unregisterTopology(new KafkaFutureImpl<>(), \"topology2\");\n            } catch (final Exception e) {\n                // unregisterTopology may throw if topology builder internals aren't fully set up;\n                // we only care about the lock ordering, not the business logic\n            }\n        }, \"thread-unregister\");\n\n        threadA.start();\n        threadB.start();\n        threadA.join();\n        threadB.join();\n    }\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":287,"cache_read_input_tokens":118978,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":287,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":6,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a146cae9-d397-4d03-b566-1cf292ffa421","uuid":"f62c404b-d5e2-43b4-97e8-59148c6ba5fd"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01Szs2hiYdZ4Q8Sv6t1Zp527","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"I see that `drainUncaughtExceptions` is called in `maybeThrowTaskExceptionsFromProcessingThreads`, which is called in the `commit` flow. After draining exceptions, it throws them. So the exception propagates up and presumably triggers error handling.\n\nBut after the exceptions are cleared, the tasks would need to be signaled to become processable again. Let me check what happens after the `commit` call...\n\nActually, the exception is thrown, which means the `commit` fails. The caller (StreamThread's process loop) handles the exception. It might trigger a task failure or rebalance. In either case, the system doesn't expect the task to become processable - it's being handled.\n \nSo the flow is: `commit()` drains the exceptions, then `maybeThrowTaskExceptions()` throws the first one, causing the commit to fail and the exception to bubble up to StreamThread. The caller then handles it by removing the failed task or triggering a rebalance, while the waiting executors will eventually be signaled when new tasks are added.\n\nBut here's the issue: if the task stays in the registry after its exception is cleared, it becomes processable again according to the `awaitProcessableTasks()` check, but no executor gets notified because they're all blocked in `await()`. The key insight is that once `isAlive()` returns false, the JVM guarantees that all the thread's actions have completed—there's a happens-before relationship that ensures this. So if I see the thread is dead, I know for certain it's finished executing, and I can safely take back the future and complete it.\n\nThe logic breaks down into two cases: either the finally block already handled the cleanup and completed the future, or the future was set after the finally block ran and is now orphaned. In both scenarios, checking `isAlive()` and completing the future is safe—if it's already done, the call is just a no-op. The real issue is making sure I'm checking at the right moment in the existing code.\n\nThe problem is that even with a second check after signaling, there's still a window where the thread could die between the initial check and the signal, or between the signal and the second check. The signal itself might get lost if the thread is already dead.\n\nBut actually, if the thread dies between the signal and the second `isAlive()` check, that second check will catch it and complete the future. And if the thread is still alive at the second check, it's guaranteed to either process the request through `handleTaskReleaseRequested()` or hit the finally block where it cleans up with `getAndSet(null)`. The only remaining concern is if the thread dies after the second check returns true, but at that point we've verified it's alive and we're relying on it to handle the request before exiting. handles request\n\nIn both cases, the request is handled. The only way it's NOT handled is if the `getAndSet(null)` in the finally block returns null (request was set after getAndSet). But the thread is still alive at the second check, meaning it hasn't executed `getAndSet(null)` yet. So when it does, it will get the future.\n\nWait, that's not right either. The second `isAlive()` returning true means the thread is alive AT THAT MOMENT. It could be:\n- Still in the while loop → will handle request\n- In the finally block, BEFORE `getAndSet` → will handle request\n- In the finally block, AFTER `getAndSet` but before thread death → WON'T handle request!\n\nThe last case is the problem. The `getAndSet` happened and returned null (because we set the future after it). The thread is still alive but it's just logging in the finally block. The second `isAlive()` returns true, so we think the thread will handle it. But it won't.\n\nThe double-check doesn't actually solve the race. The real solution is to add a flag that gets set after the `getAndSet` in the finally block, then in `unassign` after the CAS succeeds, check that flag. If it's already set, we complete the future ourselves since the executor thread won't.\n\nStill writing executor logic... I'm working through the remaining race conditions to verify the synchronization logic handles all cases correctly. Race 4 is starting but incomplete, so let me trace through what happens when the executor completes the future before the main thread even attempts to assign it.\n\nThe key insight is that the double-check pattern with getAndSet combined with the finalCheckDone flag ensures that exactly one thread successfully completes the future, regardless of timing. This covers all the edge cases where the executor and main thread race to claim and complete the future.\n\nBut I'm wondering if there's a simpler approach that doesn't require this complexity. What if I just use synchronization or a lock to prevent the race entirely? Or maybe the executor's finally block could handle the request completion in a way that's inherently race-free.\n\nActually, the simplest fix might be to add a check in unassign after signaling the executors—if the thread is already dead and the future isn't done yet, just complete it right there. Though I need to think through whether that actually prevents the race condition...\n\nThe key insight is understanding Java's memory model here. When `isAlive()` returns false, it means the thread's `run()` method has fully completed, which includes all the actions in the finally block. So if I check `isAlive()` and it's false, I can be confident that any `getAndSet` operations in the finally block have already happened.\n\nThis means the fix for `unassign()` is straightforward: after the CAS succeeds, if `isAlive()` returns false, I can safely complete the future since either the thread already handled it or it's been orphaned and needs cleanup. If the thread is still alive, I signal normally.\n\nBut there's a critical race condition: the thread could die between `isAlive()` returning true and actually processing the signal. If our CAS happened after the executor's `getAndSet` in the finally block, the future gets orphaned while `isAlive()` still reported true, so we signal but nobody's listening. The caller hangs waiting for a future that will never complete.\n\nI need to handle this specific race where the thread dies after we check it's alive but before it processes our request.\n\nFixing the race with a sentinel... Completing the sentinel logic...\n\nThe sentinel approach works cleanly: when the executor thread exits, it sets a completed sentinel that blocks any new CAS operations. If a release request comes in after the thread has died, the CAS fails because the value isn't null anymore, and we detect the sentinel by checking if the current value is already done—then we complete the future immediately instead of throwing an error. In the normal case where the executor is still alive, the CAS succeeds and the signal gets processed as expected. The sentinel approach is bulletproof—it atomically handles the race between the executor completing a pending request and `unassign()` trying to set a new one. Since `getAndSet` is atomic, either the CAS succeeds first and the executor completes the future, or the sentinel is already set and the CAS fails. There's no window for both operations to interleave dangerously.\n\nNow I'm realizing there's a wrinkle with how `handleTaskReleaseRequested()` resets the field back to null during normal operation—once we set the sentinel in the finally block, that field stays sentinel permanently since the executor thread is shutting down anyway. The question is whether the `isAlive()` check elsewhere accounts for this state change. the case where `unassign()` is called when the executor thread is null—in that scenario, we just complete the future immediately since there's no thread to signal. Now let me put this together: I need to update the finally block in the thread's run method to use the sentinel value, and then modify `unassign()` to detect when the CAS fails because the sentinel is already in place.\n\nImplementing the changes... executor dies at the exact right moment\n     - The finally block does `taskReleaseRequested.getAndSet(null)` — if `unassign()` sets the future AFTER this but BEFORE `isAlive()` returns false, the (`/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/DefaultStateUpdater.java`)\n     - 3 locks: `tasksAndActionsLock`, `restoredActiveTasksLock`, `exceptionsAndFailedTasksLock`\n     - `executeWithQueuesLocked()` acquires all 3 in consistent order: tasksAndActions → restoredActiveTasks → exceptionsAndFailed\n     - Individual methods acquire single locks — consistent with the 3-lock ordering\n     - `waitIfAllChangelogsCompletelyRead()` uses `tasksAndActionsCondition.await()` in proper while loop\n     - No deadlock or hang found in this file\n\n   - **ThreadCache.java** future.complete(null);\n   291→            } else {\n   292→                taskManager.signalTaskExecutors();\n   293→            }\n   294→        } else {\n   295→            log.debug(\"Tried to * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n * the License. License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\npackage org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.tasks;\n\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaFuture;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.internals.KafkaFutureImpl;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.LogContext;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Time;\nimport org.apache.kafka.streams.errors.StreamsException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.streams.errors.TaskMigratedException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.ProcessingThread;\nimport org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.ReadOnlyTask;\nimport org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.StreamTask;\nimport org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.Task;\nimport org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.TaskExecutionMetadata;\n\nimport org.slf4j.Logger;\n\nimport java.time.Duration;\nimport java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicBoolean;\nimport java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicReference;\n\nimport static org.apache.kafka.streams.internals.StreamsConfigUtils.ProcessingMode.EXACTLY_ONCE_V2;\n\npublic class DefaultTaskExecutor implements TaskExecutor {\n\n    private class TaskExecutorThread extends Thread implements ProcessingThread {\n\n        private final AtomicBoolean shutdownRequested = new AtomicBoolean(false);\n        private final AtomicReference<KafkaFutureImpl<StreamTask>> taskReleaseRequested = new AtomicReference<>(null);\n\n        private final Logger log;\n\n        public TaskExecutorThread(final String name) {\n            super(name);\n            final String logPrefix = String.format(\"%s \", name);\n            final LogContext logContext = new LogContext(logPrefix);\n            log = logContext.logger(DefaultTaskExecutor.class);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void run() {\n            log.info(\"Task executor thread started\");\n            try {\n                while (!shutdownRequested.get()) {\n                    try {\n                        runOnce(time.milliseconds());\n                    } catch (final StreamsException e) {\n                        handleException(e);\n                    } catch (final Exception e) {\n                        handleException(new StreamsException(e));\n                    }\n                }\n            } finally {\n                if (currentTask != null) {\n                    log.debug(\"Releasing task {} due to shutdown.\", currentTask.id());\n                    unassignCurrentTask();\n                }\n\n                final KafkaFutureImpl<StreamTask> taskReleaseFuture;\n                if ((taskReleaseFuture = taskReleaseRequested.getAndSet(null)) != null) {\n                    log.debug(\"Asked to return current task, but shutting down.\");\n                    taskReleaseFuture.complete(null);\n                }\n                log.info(\"Task executor thread shutdown\");\n            }\n        }\n\n        private void handleTaskReleaseRequested() {\n            final KafkaFutureImpl<StreamTask> taskReleaseFuture;\n            if ((taskReleaseFuture = taskReleaseRequested.getAndSet(null)) != null) {\n                if (currentTask != null) {\n                    log.debug(\"Releasing task {} upon request.\", currentTask.id());\n                    final StreamTask unassignedTask = unassignCurrentTask();\n                    taskReleaseFuture.complete(unassignedTask);\n                } else {\n                    log.debug(\"Asked to return current task, but returned current task already.\");\n                    taskReleaseFuture.complete(null);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        private void handleException(final StreamsException e) {\n            if (currentTask != null) {\n                taskManager.setUncaughtException(e, currentTask.id());\n\n                log.debug(\"Releasing task {} due to uncaught exception.\", currentTask.id());\n                unassignCurrentTask();\n            } else {\n                // If we do not currently have a task assigned and still get an error, this is fatal for the executor thread\n                throw e;\n            }\n        }\n\n        private void runOnce(final long nowMs) {\n            handleTaskReleaseRequested();\n\n            if (currentTask == null) {\n                currentTask = taskManager.assignNextTask(DefaultTaskExecutor.this);\n            }\n\n            if (currentTask == null) {\n                try {\n                    taskManager.awaitProcessableTasks(shutdownRequested::get);\n                } catch (final InterruptedException ignored) {\n                    // Can be ignored, the cause of the interrupted will be handled in the event loop\n                }\n            } else {\n                boolean progressed = false;\n\n                if (taskExecutionMetadata.canProcessTask(currentTask, nowMs) && currentTask.isProcessable(nowMs)) {\n                    if (processTask(currentTask, nowMs, time)) {\n                        log.trace(\"processed a record for {}\", currentTask.id());\n                        progressed = true;\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if (taskExecutionMetadata.canPunctuateTask(currentTask)) {\n                    if (currentTask.maybePunctuateStreamTime()) {\n                        log.trace(\"punctuated stream time for task {} \", currentTask.id());\n                        progressed = true;\n                    }\n                    if (currentTask.maybePunctuateSystemTime()) {\n                        log.trace(\"punctuated system time for task {} \", currentTask.id());\n                        progressed = true;\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if (!progressed) {\n                    log.debug(\"Releasing task {} because we are not making progress.\", currentTask.id());\n                    unassignCurrentTask();\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        private boolean processTask(final Task task, final long now, final Time time) {\n            boolean processed = false;\n            try {\n                processed = task.process(now);\n                if (processed) {\n                    log.trace(\"Successfully processed task {}\", task.id());\n                    task.clearTaskTimeout();\n                    // TODO: enable regardless of whether using named topologies\n                    if (taskExecutionMetadata.hasNamedTopologies() && taskExecutionMetadata.processingMode() != EXACTLY_ONCE_V2) {\n                        taskExecutionMetadata.addToSuccessfullyProcessed(task);\n                    }\n                }\n            } catch (final TimeoutException timeoutException) {\n                // TODO consolidate TimeoutException retries with general error handling\n                task.maybeInitTaskTimeoutOrThrow(now, timeoutException);\n                log.error(\n                    String.format(\n                        \"Could not complete processing records for %s due to the following exception; will move to next task and retry later\",\n                        task.id()),\n                    timeoutException\n                );\n            } catch (final TaskMigratedException e) {\n                log.info(\"Failed to process stream task {} since it got migrated to another thread already. \" +\n                    \"Will trigger a new rebalance and close all tasks as zombies together.\", task.id());\n                throw e;\n            } catch (final StreamsException e) {\n                log.error(String.format(\"Failed to process stream task %s due to the following error:\", task.id()), e);\n                e.setTaskId(task.id());\n                throw e;\n            } catch (final RuntimeException e) {\n                log.error(String.format(\"Failed to process stream task %s due to the following error:\", task.id()), e);\n                throw new StreamsException(e, task.id());\n            } finally {\n                task.recordProcessBatchTime(time.milliseconds() - now);\n            }\n            return processed;\n        }\n\n        private StreamTask unassignCurrentTask() {\n            if (currentTask == null)\n                throw new IllegalStateException(\"Does not own any task while being ask to unassign from task manager\");\n\n            // flush the task before giving it back to task manager, if we are not handing it back because of an error.\n            if (!taskManager.hasUncaughtException(currentTask.id())) {\n                try {\n                    currentTask.flush();\n                } catch (final StreamsException e) {\n                    log.error(String.format(\"Failed to flush stream task %s due to the following error:\", currentTask.id()), e);\n                    e.setTaskId(currentTask.id());\n                    taskManager.setUncaughtException(e, currentTask.id());\n                } catch (final RuntimeException e) {\n                    log.error(String.format(\"Failed to flush stream task %s due to the following error:\", currentTask.id()), e);\n                    taskManager.setUncaughtException(new StreamsException(e, currentTask.id()), currentTask.id());\n                }\n            }\n            taskManager.unassignTask(currentTask, DefaultTaskExecutor.this);\n\n            final StreamTask retTask = currentTask;\n            currentTask = null;\n            return retTask;\n        }\n    }\n\n    private final Time time;\n    private final String name;\n    private final TaskManager taskManager;\n    private final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata;\n    private final Logger log;\n\n    private StreamTask currentTask = null;\n    private TaskExecutorThread taskExecutorThread = null;\n\n    public DefaultTaskExecutor(final TaskManager taskManager,\n                               final String name,\n                               final Time time,\n                               final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata) {\n        this.time = time;\n        this.name = name;\n        this.taskManager = taskManager;\n        this.taskExecutionMetadata = taskExecutionMetadata;\n        final LogContext logContext = new LogContext(name);\n        this.log = logContext.logger(DefaultTaskExecutor.class);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public String name() {\n        return name;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void start() {\n        if (taskExecutorThread == null) {\n            taskExecutorThread = new TaskExecutorThread(name);\n            taskExecutorThread.start();\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public boolean isRunning() {\n        return taskExecutorThread != null && taskExecutorThread.isAlive();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void requestShutdown() {\n        if (taskExecutorThread != null) {\n            taskExecutorThread.shutdownRequested.set(true);\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void awaitShutdown(final Duration timeout) {\n        if (taskExecutorThread != null) {\n            try {\n                taskExecutorThread.join(timeout.toMillis());\n                if (taskExecutorThread.isAlive()) {\n                    throw new StreamsException(\"State updater thread did not shutdown within the timeout\");\n                }\n                taskExecutorThread = null;\n            } catch (final InterruptedException ignored) {\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public ReadOnlyTask currentTask() {\n        return currentTask != null ? new ReadOnlyTask(currentTask) : null;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public KafkaFuture<StreamTask> unassign() {\n        final KafkaFutureImpl<StreamTask> future = new KafkaFutureImpl<>();\n\n        if (taskExecutorThread != null) {\n            log.debug(\"Asking {} to hand back task\", taskExecutorThread.getName());\n            if (!taskExecutorThread.taskReleaseRequested.compareAndSet(null, future)) {\n                throw new IllegalStateException(\"There was already a task release request registered\");\n            }\n            if (!taskExecutorThread.isAlive()) {\n                log.debug(\"Completing future, because task executor was just shut down\");\n                future.complete(null);\n            } else {\n                taskManager.signalTaskExecutors();\n            }\n        } else {\n            log.debug(\"Tried to unassign but no thread is running\");\n            future.complete(null);\n        }\n\n        return future;\n    }\n}\n","numLines":302,"startLine":1,"totalLines":302}}}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_0191AZ2emP7qKSD3FpxTRomN","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Now let me analyze the `unassign()` method carefully for the race condition:\n\n1. `unassign()` is called from `DefaultTaskManager.lockTasks()` while holding `tasksLock`\n2. It creates a `KafkaFutureImpl<StreamTask>` future\n3. It does `taskReleaseRequested.compareAndSet(null, future)` to set the future\n4. Then checks `!taskExecutorThread.isAlive()` - if dead, completes future immediately\n5. Otherwise signals executors\n\nThe race:\n- The executor thread's `run()` method has a finally block:\n\n\n- The finally block retrieves and clears any pending release request, completing the future if one exists\n- Here's the problematic interleaving: the executor thread executes `getAndSet(null)` and gets null because there's no pending request yet, then `unassign()` runs and successfully sets the future via `compareAndSet()` — but now the executor thread has already passed the point where it checks for release requests, so the future never gets completed\n- The executor thread then checks if it's still alive (which it is, since it's still in the finally block), so `unassign()` calls `signalTaskExecutors()` instead of completing the future directly\n- This is a genuine race condition: the future will never complete, causing `lockTasks()` to hang forever on `future.get()`\n\nLet me trace through the finally block logic more carefully...\n\nThe thread retrieves any pending task release future and completes it before exiting. The real issue is the window between when `getAndSet(null)` clears the future and when the thread actually dies — during this gap, `unassign()` can set a new future and incorrectly think the thread is still alive, so it signals executors instead of completing the future.\n\nThe straightforward fix would be to have `unassign()` check if the thread is alive after setting the future and handle completion if needed, but that still leaves a race. A more solid solution would be to synchronize the thread termination with future completion or use a different mechanism to ensure the future always gets resolved. use a sentinel value or a shutdown flag that prevents new futures from being set after the executor starts shutting down.\n\nActually, let me think about this differently. The problem is:\n\n1. `taskReleaseRequested.getAndSet(null)` in finally block (reads null, no future pending)\n2. `compareAndSet(null, future)` in unassign() succeeds\n3. `isAlive()` returns true (thread still in finally block)\n4. Thread exits - future is orphaned\n\nThe fix could be:\n- In the finally block, after `getAndSet(null)`, set it to some sentinel that causes `compareAndSet(null, future)` to fail\n- Or, use `shutdownRequested` more carefully\n\nActually the simplest fix: In the finally block of `run()`, instead of `getAndSet(null)`, we should use a different approach. We could set a completed sentinel future in `taskReleaseRequested` after processing it, so subsequent `compareAndSet(null, future)` calls fail. Then in `unassign()`, if the compare-and-set fails and the thread is no longer alive, we complete the future immediately. But the race condition is that `isAlive()` still returns true while the thread is exiting the finally block.\n\nThe cleanest solution is probably to loop in `unassign()` with a retry mechanism...\n\nLooping in `unassign()` with retry logic... I'm seeing a race condition where the executor thread could die between our `isAlive()` check and when it actually exits, leaving the future orphaned if it was set during that window. The real solution is to use a sentinel value or atomic flag that prevents this race entirely, rather than trying to patch it with timing checks. Still writing the synchronization logic...\n\nActually, I'm realizing the sentinel approach has a flaw—I need to distinguish between a CAS failure due to an existing request versus one due to shutdown. Using a simple `shutdownRequested` boolean flag would be cleaner than trying to parse the state from the atomic reference.\n\nNow I'm working through the race condition where the thread might shut down right after we set the future. The idea is to check if the thread is still alive after the CAS succeeds, and if it's dead, try to reclaim the future ourselves since the executor's cleanup might have already consumed it or might miss it entirely. there (CAS succeeds). But wait, I need to think through this more carefully. If the finally block already ran `getAndSet(null)` and consumed our future, then our CAS would fail since the reference is now null instead of holding our future. The real issue is that between checking `isAlive()` and doing the CAS, the thread's finally block could have already executed and cleared the reference, leaving our future orphaned. With the fix of checking `isAlive()` first, if it returns true, we know the finally block hasn't run yet, so our CAS will succeed and the future will be processed.\n\nActually, that's still not quite right. The thread could be alive at the `isAlive()` check but then die before processing the future we just set. The real solution is to use a sentinel value in the finally block so that `compareAndSet(null, future)` fails if the finally block has already run, preventing us from setting a future that won't be processed.\n\nSetting up the sentinel approach...\n\nImplementing the sentinel in the finally block...\n\nActually, there's a simpler approach here. The `handleTaskReleaseRequested` method also uses `getAndSet(null)`, which could have its own race condition, but that's called from within the main loop so it's safe. The real issue is what happens in the executor's finally block when it tries to complete the future.\n\nThe sentinel approach is the cleanest fix—it prevents any new futures from being set after shutdown. But I could also check `shutdownRequested` directly in `unassign()` to avoid setting a future that won't be completed. I'm reconsidering the approach — instead of trying to handle the race condition with complex state checks, I could use a spin-loop that keeps draining any release requests until the reference stays null, ensuring we don't miss any futures that get set while we're completing the current one. Still writing the fix...\n\nActually, I realize there's a race condition here—after setting the sentinel, the thread might still be alive briefly, so checking `isAlive()` would incorrectly throw an exception. Instead, I should check if the current value is the sentinel itself, which cleanly handles the shutdown case without relying on thread state timing. I'm defining a static sentinel value that both the executor thread and the unassign method can reference to signal shutdown. Now I need to trace through what happens during shutdown—specifically when `handleTaskReleaseRequested` is called from the main loop after `shutdownRequested` becomes true, to make sure the state transitions work correctly.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4160,"cache_read_input_tokens":31522,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":4160,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a146cae9-d397-4d03-b566-1cf292ffa421","uuid":"750ffe47-cb99-4dfb-a715-d25be0c05937"}

private final AtomicReference<KafkaFutureImpl<StreamTask>> taskReleaseRequested = new AtomicReference<>(null);\n\n        private final Logger log;\n\n        public TaskExecutorThread(final String name) {\n            super(name);\n            final String logPrefix = String.format(\"%s \", name);\n            final LogContext logContext = new LogContext(logPrefix);\n            log =
